entropy.interpretation: Average m-subset depth-w fiber is ~2^(-18820): the natural finite criterion is M_m := max_b |Fib_w^{(m)}(b)| is 0 or 1 (or tiny). Anticode is ~2^1.69e6 — useless for the atom; entropy is the signal.

criterion_Mm_Ures.name: residual_atom_from_Mm_and_Ures
criterion_Mm_Ures.note: Entropy makes M_m^{max}<=1 the expected truth at deployed (m,w,p); proving it is a sharp prefix-fiber uniqueness statement for m-subsets.
open_inputs: OPEN: prove M_m^{max} <= 1 (or any bound <= 2^10 is already powerful)
OPEN: prove U_res(z) <= target/pack (or <= t*p/pack for D_prim form)
proof: Apply v4: |R| <= pack * N_can_prim <= pack * (target/pack) = target.
criterion_Mm_Ures.statement: If M_m^{max} := max_b |Fib_w^{(m)}(b)| <= 1 and max_z U_res(z) <= floor(target_floor/pack_ceil), then max_z N_can_prim(z) <= floor(target_floor/pack_ceil), hence max_z |R(z)| <= target_floor by residual lex covering (v4), which is the K_rem residual flatness form.

full_fiber_routing.name: full_fiber_N_can_same_routing
proof: Identical to residual routing without restricting the domain.
full_fiber_routing.statement: The same side-prefix routing holds for full Fib_w(z): N_can(z) <= U_full(z) * M_m(z). Path A is identical algebra with R=Fib.

residual_core_routing.name: residual_can_core_side_prefix_routing
proof: v3/v4: triangular inversion under m>=w gives b as a function of (z,u).
C is an m-subset whose monic locator has prefix b, i.e. C in Fib_w^{(m)}(b).
Partition residual can-cores by the side-prefix u of their defining S. Each block has size <= M_m(z). Number of blocks <= U_res(z).
residual_core_routing.statement: Let R(z) be any subset of Fib_w(z) (e.g. first-match residual). For S in R(z) let U be the e=w+1 smallest-exponent elements of S, C=S\\U, and let u be the first w monic coefficients of Lambda_U. Let b=b(z,u) be the triangular core prefix from v3. Then C lies in the m-subset fiber Fib_w^{(m)}(b). Consequently, writing U_res(z) for the number of distinct residual side-prefixes u and M_m(z) = max_u |Fib_w^{(m)}(b(z,u))|, one has N_can_prim(z) <= U_res(z) * M_m(z).

side_prefix_pencil.name: e_subset_fixed_w_prefix_is_constant_shift_pencil
proof: Monic degree e=w+1: fixing the first w monic coefficients leaves only the constant term free — that is the definition of a constant-shift pencil.
Root sets of distinct fully split members are pairwise disjoint; pack into D.
side_prefix_pencil.statement: Fix w and e=w+1. The e-subsets U whose monic locators share a fixed length-w coefficient prefix u form a constant-shift family (varying only the constant term). Hence there are at most floor(n/e) such U.

open_program.A_same_with_full_U: Same routing for full-fiber N_can with U_full instead of U_res.
open_program.B1_prove_Mm_max: Prove max_b |Fib_w^{(m)}(b)| is tiny at n=2^21,m=913632,w=67471,p=KoalaBear. Entropy: log2 avg ≈ -18820.25.
open_program.B2_prove_U_res: Prove max residual side-prefix count U_res(z) <= 16166878605395467 (or <= 8456648496904 for t*p form).

title: Residual N_can_prim via m-fiber routing and M_m criterion
